intro: Exacum divaricatum, also known as the Indian snakeroot or purple bugloss, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Exacum divaricatum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Exacum divaricatum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, and is also used as a digestive aid to relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
